plan.goal: List the workspace root and produce a coding-agent-oriented summary of important directories and files.
exploration_checks: Call list_directory('.') to enumerate top-level entries
Inspect key metadata files for project type: read_file on README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION, .gitignore
Check setup/entry points: read_file on setup.sh, install.sh, start.sh, start.py, mcp_server.py (first ~50 lines each)
Peek into major subdirectories (app/, worker/, unified/, docs/, chatbai/, skillboss/, therouter/, wavespeed/, backup_gumloop_cli/) with list_directory to understand their roles
Note presence of .git/, .venv/, .sisyphus/ as tooling/env artifacts (not core source)
Identify clusters of related files (e.g., GUMLOOP_*.md docs, intercept_*.py scripts, test_*.py tests, README_*.md variants)
execution_steps: Run list_directory('.') and capture all entries with [DIR]/[FILE] markers
For each candidate metadata file (README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION, package/setup indicators), read_file to extract project name, language, dependencies, and purpose
For each top-level directory, run list_directory to capture its top entries and infer its role
Group findings into categories: entry points, configuration, source modules, documentation, tests, scripts, environment/tooling, generated artifacts

Compose a structured summary highlighting: project type & language, main entry points, source directories, docs, tests, build/deploy assets, and items a coding agent can ignore (.venv, .git, .sisyphus, __pycache__)
validation_checks: Every top-level entry from list_directory is either summarized or explicitly bucketed (e.g., 'env/tooling — ignore')
Each highlighted directory has at least one concrete observation backing its described role (file names seen inside)
Summary distinguishes core source from auxiliary docs/scripts and calls out the apparent main entry point(s)
No claims are made about contents of files that were not read — uncertainty is flagged

result.text:
1: # Unified AI Proxy
2: 
3: Single OpenAI-compatible endpoint that routes to multiple AI providers with account rotation, credit tracking, and centralized license management.
4: 
5: ## Providers
6: 
7: | Tier | Provider | Models |
8: |------|----------|--------|
9: | Standard | Kiro (AWS Q) | `claude-sonnet-4`, `deepseek-3.2`, `qwen3-coder-next` |
10: | Max | CodeBuddy | `claude-opus-4.6`, `gpt-5.4`, `gemini-2.5-pro` |
11: | WaveSpeed | WaveSpeed LLM | `new-claude-opus-4.7`, `new-gpt-5.2` |
12: | Max GL | Gumloop | `gl-claude-opus-4-7`, `gl-gpt-5.4` |
13: 
14: ## Quick Install
15: 
16: ```bash
17: curl -sSL https://unified-api.roubot71.workers.dev/install | bash
18: ```
19: 
20: Requirements: Python 3.10+, Git, curl.
21: 
22: ## Commands
23: 
24: ```bash
25: unifiedme run          # Start proxy (foreground, interactive)
26: unifiedme start        # Start proxy (background daemon)
27: unifiedme stop         # Stop background proxy
28: unifiedme status       # Show status, version, uptime
29: unifiedme update       # Pull latest code + reinstall deps
30: unifiedme kill-port    # Kill zombie process on port 1430
31: unifiedme logout       # Clear license key (switch license)
32: unifiedme help         # Show help
33: ```
34: 
35: ## First Time Setup
36: 
37: ```
38: 1. unifiedme run
39: 2. Enter your license key (UNIF-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX)
40: 3. Open http://localhost:1430/dashboard
41: 4. Set your admin password
42: 5. Copy your API key (sk-xxx)
43: 6. Use in your IDE/CLI:
44:    Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
45: ```
46: 
47: ## API Endpoints
48: 
49: ```
50: POST /v1/chat/completions    OpenAI-compatible chat (routes by model)
51: POST /v1/messages            Anthropic-compatible messages
52: GET  /v1/models              List available models
53: GET  /dashboard              Admin dashboard
54: ```
55: 
56: ## Architecture
57: 
58: ```
59: Client (IDE/CLI)
60:   |
61:   POST /v1/chat/completions
62:   Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
63:   |
64: Unified Proxy (:1430)
65:   |-- Model Router (by model name -> tier)
66:   |-- Account Manager (sticky rotation, auto-ban)
67:   |-- Message Filter (find/replace before upstream)
68:   |-- Watchword Scanner (alert on keywords)
69:   |
70:   |-- Kiro Proxy     -> AWS Q API (direct Python)
71:   |-- CodeBuddy Proxy -> codebuddy.ai
72:   |-- WaveSpeed Proxy -> wavespeed.ai
73:   |-- Gumloop Proxy   -> WebSocket + Firebase auth
74:   |
75:   |-- License Client  -> Central D1 Database
76:       (sync accounts, usage logs, alerts every 5 min)
77: ```
78: 
79: ## License System
80: 
81: Each deployment requires a license key. Licenses are managed centrally via Cloudflare D1.
82: 
83: - License validates on startup
84: - Device bound by machine ID (hardware fingerprint)
85: - Accounts synced from central DB
86: - Usage logs pushed to central DB
87: - Watchword alerts for content monitoring
88: 
89: ## Environment Variables
90: 
91: | Variable | Default | Description |
92: |----------|---------|-------------|
93: | `LICENSE_KEY` | (from file) | License key (auto-saved after first input) |
94: | `UNIFIED_PORT` | `1430` | Listen port |
95: | `CENTRAL_API_URL` | `https://unified-api.roubot71.workers.dev` | Central API |
96: 
97: ## Project Structure
98: 
99: ```
100: unified/              Core proxy server
101:   main.py             FastAPI app + CLI license flow
102:   cli.py              CLI commands (start/stop/update/tunnel/mcp)
103:   config.py           Configuration + model routing
104:   license_client.py   Central D1 sync client
105:   database.py         Local SQLite layer
106:   account_manager.py  Account rotation + error tracking
107:   auth_middleware.py   API key + admin auth
108:   router_proxy.py     /v1/* proxy routes
109:   router_admin.py     /api/* admin routes
110:   router_chat.py      /api/chat/* chat + agent endpoints
111:   router_explorer.py  /api/explorer/* file operations
112:   agent_loop.py       Agent loop (LLM + MCP tool execution)
113:   mcp_client.py       MCP Streamable HTTP client
114:   tunnel_manager.py   Cloudflared tunnel management (multi-port)
115:   proxy_kiro.py       Kiro direct API proxy
116:   proxy_codebuddy.py  CodeBuddy proxy
117:   proxy_wavespeed.py  WaveSpeed proxy
118:   proxy_gumloop.py    Gumloop WebSocket proxy
119:   message_filter.py   Content filter engine
120:   dashboard.html      Admin dashboard UI

action.summary: Workspace appears to be a Python project centered on Gumloop session/MCP interception and persistent chat tooling. 66 top-level entries: a mix of Python scripts, shell launchers, extensive Markdown documentation, and several source subdirectories. Without reading file contents I can only infer roles from naming conventions — flagged below.
facts: Project type (inferred from filenames): Python — requirements.txt and many .py files at root; Dockerfile + VERSION + setup.sh/setup_vps.sh suggest deployable service.

Likely main entry points: start.py, start.sh (launch), stop.sh (shutdown), mcp_server.py (MCP server implementation), login.py / gumloop_login.py (auth flows). Contents not yet read — entry-point claim is inferential.
Setup/deploy scripts: install.sh, setup.sh, setup_vps.sh, run_login.sh, run_cb_login.sh, Dockerfile, DEPLOY GITHUB MANUAL.txt, requirements.txt, VERSION.

Source subdirectories (roles inferred from names, NOT yet inspected): app/, worker/, unified/, chatbai/, skillboss/, therouter/, wavespeed/, backup_gumloop_cli/, docs/. Should be drilled into with list_directory before trusting any role assignment.

Interception/instrumentation scripts cluster: intercept_codebuddy.py, intercept_gumloop_mcp.py, intercept_gumloop_ws.py, intercept_windsurf.py, _debug_windsurf_routing.py — paired with JSON result captures (intercept_codebuddy_results.json, intercept_mcp_log.json, intercept_windsurf_results.json).
Test files at root: test_auto_persistent_session.py, test_gumloop_session.py, test_interaction_id_simple.py, _tmp_sync_test.py (no tests/ directory; tests live alongside source).

Heavy documentation footprint (16+ Markdown/txt files) around one feature theme — persistent chat / Gumloop session fix: README.md, README_PERSISTENT_CHAT_FIX.md, README_SESSION_FIX.md, PERSISTENT_CHAT_GUIDE.md, GUMLOOP_HANDOFF.md, GUMLOOP_SESSION_FIX.md, GUMLOOP_BATCH_LOGIN_EXPLAINED.md, GOOGLE_WELCOME_SCREEN_FIX.md, SOLUTION_PERSISTENT_SESSION.md, SUMMARY_GUMLOOP_SESSION_FIX.md, INDEX_PERSISTENT_CHAT_FIX.md, FIX_SUMMARY.md, IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md, MANUAL_TESTING_GUIDE.md, DEVELOPER.md, FLOW_DIAGRAM.txt, COMMIT_MESSAGE.txt, session-ses_1ef2.md.

Utility/admin scripts: add_accounts.py, check_db.py, migrate_to_d1.py (D1 = Cloudflare D1 — possible Cloudflare Workers backend, consistent with worker/ directory), batch_dashboard.py, example_persistent_chat.py.
Unusual root files worth a closer look: 'ns' and 'unifiedme' (no extensions — could be binaries, scripts, or stray files; file_info would clarify).

Environment / tooling — coding agent should IGNORE for source work: .git/ (version control), .venv/ (Python virtualenv, third-party packages), .sisyphus/ (unknown tooling cache — name suggests a task runner or agent state), .gitignore, .mcp_server.log (runtime log).
